root.description: Absolute path of the scanned root at generation time.

commitSha.description: Commit SHA of the scanned tree; null when not a git repo.

dirty.description: True when the working tree had uncommitted changes at scan time.

fileHashes.description: Per-file content hashes (relative posix path → sha256), enabling an incremental re-scan (6.1) to detect exactly which files changed since this graph was produced. Absent on graphs scanned without `--update`/`--watch`.

GraphMeta.description: Scan provenance — which code this graph describes.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

exportName.description: Named or default export, if exported.

props.description: Prop names destructured or accessed from the props object.

renderedText.description: Static text visible in the rendered output — the primary signal for matching a screenshot to a component. i18n keys are expanded to one entry per locale, so a French screenshot matches the same component.

rendersComponents.description: Names of components this component renders in its JSX (deduplicated).

structure.description: Structural fingerprint of the rendered subtree (Phase 4.2).

ComponentNode.description: A React component definition — the code, not a usage.

file.description: Path relative to the scan root, POSIX separators.

SourceLocation.description: Where a node lives in the codebase.

source.description: Where the text comes from: JSX children, a string attribute (placeholder/label/title/alt/aria-label), an i18n key resolved against locale files, or portal-rendered content (toast("Order deleted") — the text appears far from the caller in the DOM, but the CALLER is the component a screenshot of it should match).

key.description: i18n entries only: the translation key, e.g. "team.title".

locale.description: i18n entries only: which locale this text belongs to.

branch.description: Condition source text when the text renders only in a branch.

template.description: True when the text came from a template literal with runtime parts — unknown segments appear as `*` ("* items in cart") and match as wildcards.

RenderedText.description: One piece of text a component can render, with its provenance.

columns.description: Column headers — the max `<th>` (or design-system column) count in a table.

StructuralSignature.description: Counts of the structural elements a component renders — the signal for matching a screenshot with little or no static text (TRACKER step 4.2, failure modes A1/A3/A12). Raw DOM tags plus common design-system aliases (`<Table>`, `<TextField>`, …) are folded into these buckets.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

definitionId.description: Id of the ComponentNode this instantiates.

parentInstanceId.description: Enclosing instance in the render tree. Null until the cross-file instance tree is built (Phase 2.1); the enclosing *definition* is available via the incoming `renders` edge meanwhile.

staticProps.description: Props with statically-known string values at this call site.

InstanceNode.description:
A component as rendered at one specific call site.

The same <DataTable> definition rendered on the Users page and the Invoices page yields two instances — and per-instance data attribution (Phase 2.2) is what lets each report a different API.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

HookNode.description: A custom hook — often the bridge between a component and its data.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

endpoint.description: Canonical endpoint pattern: constants folded, template placeholders normalized to :param form — e.g. "/api/users/:id". This is the value attribution and cross-graph joins match on.

raw.description: The endpoint expression exactly as written in source.

queryKey.description: react-query/SWR cache key as written in source (e.g. `["users"]`) — the identity used for cache-sharing analysis (C7) alongside the endpoint.

responseType.description: The response shape this source returns, when recoverable (5.5, F4).

DataSourceNode.description: An external data origin: an HTTP endpoint, GraphQL operation, or socket.

EndpointResolution.description: How much of an endpoint was statically resolvable.

name.description: Named type when resolvable ("User", "User[]"); otherwise the type text.

source.description: Where the type was recovered from.

ResponseType.description: The shape a data source returns (TRACKER step 5.4→5.5, failure mode F4). Resolved from a call's generic argument (`useQuery<User[]>`), a type annotation on the variable the result lands in, or an OpenAPI spec matched by endpoint. One level deep only — fields, not their nested shapes.

ResponseField.description: One field of a response type — a name and its type as written.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

StateNode.description: Local or global state a component reads.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

handler.description: Name of the handler function, if resolvable.

source.description: Where the binding comes from (TRACKER step 3.4): a JSX on* prop (the default when absent), a form-library submit handler (react-hook-form's `handleSubmit`, Formik), an `addEventListener` inside an effect, or a hotkey-library registration (`useHotkeys`).

EventNode.description: A user or system event a component responds to.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

path.description: Route pattern with dynamic segments in :param form — "/users/:id", catch-alls as ":param*". Matches the endpoint-pattern convention so navigate("/users/" + id) effects (step 3.2) join on the same shape.

layout.description: Name of the layout component wrapping this route's page (a pathless layout route in React Router; the nearest layout.tsx in Next.js app router). Null when the page renders bare.

guards.description: Guard components between the route and its page — auth/role wrappers like <RequireAuth> around the element or as pathless ancestor routes.

RouteNode.description: A URL route and the page component it renders (TRACKER step 3.1, failure mode B4). Routes are journey-graph entry points: a journey step like "navigate to /users/:id" resolves through the route to the page component's instance tree.

RouterKind.description: Which routing system declared a route.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

url.description: Destination as written — a URL, a `mailto:`/`tel:` scheme, or "inbound".

host.description: Host or scheme used to group destinations (accounts.google.com, mailto).

ExternalNode.description: A destination outside this codebase (TRACKER failure mode B9): an OAuth provider, a payment gateway, a `mailto:`/`tel:` link, or the inbound side of a deep-link/OAuth-callback route. Journeys that reach one leave the app.

id.description: Stable id, unique within a graph. See nodeId()/instanceId().

flags.description: Degradation markers, e.g. "incomplete", "depth-limited", "unresolved-prop-handler", "external-definition". Absent when clean.

framework.description: Which runner the file appears to use, by its imports/globals.

TestNode.description: A test file that exercises one or more components (TRACKER step 5.4, failure mode F3). Linked to the components it renders by `covered-by` edges, so the context bundle can name the tests that guard a change — and flag components that have none.

via.description: Prop name for provides-data edges; handler name for triggers.

expression.description: Source text of the condition, e.g. `isEnabled("new-billing")`.

EdgeCondition.description: A statically-detected condition guarding an edge (feature flag, role, branch).
